message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>For many institutions of higher learning, the beginning of each semester is marked by a significant migration of young adults into the area. In the midst of the COVID19 pandemic, this presents an opportunity for active cases to be introduced into a community. Prior to the Fall 2020 semester, Colorado State University researchers combined student home locations with recent case counts compiled by the New York Times to assign a probability to each individual of arriving with COVID19. These probabilities were combined to estimate that there would be 7.8 new cases among the on-campus population. Comprehensive testing of arriving students revealed 7 new cases, which validated the approach. The procedure was repeated to explore what could happen if students had returned to campus after Fall break. The estimate of 48 cases corroborated the University’s early decision to transition to fully remote learning after break.</jats:p>
title: Estimating COVID-19 Cases on University Campuses Prior To Semester

reference.unstructured: A. J. Leidner , MMWR. Morbidity and Mortality Weekly Report 70, issn: 0149-21951545-861X, (2021; https://www.cdc.gov/mmwr/volumes/70/wr/mm7001a4.htm) (2021).
The New York Times. (2020). Coronavirus (Covid-19) Data in the United States. Retrieved Jan. 1 2021, from https://github.com/nytimes/covid-19-data.

U.S. Census Bureau. (2020). 2014-2019 American Community Survey 5-year Summary File. Retrieved Jan 7, 2021, from https://www2.census.gov/programs-surveys/acs/summary_file/2019/data/5_year_entire_sf/Tracts_Block_Groups_Only.zip
U.S. Department of Housing and Urban Development. (2020). USPS ZIP Code Crosswalk Files. Retrieved Dec 31, 2020, from https://www.huduser.gov/portal/datasets/usps_crosswalk.html
